title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, will run the Boston Marathon to honor her mother and raise awareness for early-onset Alzheimer's.
Wright's family has been heavily affected by Alzheimer's, with multiple relatives, including her mother, diagnosed and lost to the disease.
Running with the Alzheimer's Association, Wright aims to increase research funding and support for caregivers and affected families.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Samantha Pelrine, a Plymouth police officer, was suspended after being charged with child rape alongside her husband, Daniel Forand.
The Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission ordered Pelrine to surrender her police equipment following her felony charge.
The alleged victim, now an adult, claimed abuse occurred between 2018 and 2025 while Pelrine and Forand were his guardians.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police warn residents after seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, citing potential for tragedy if trend continues.
The latest incident involved a 15-year-old losing control of an e-bike and colliding with a car, with no reported injuries.
Massachusetts law treats e-bikes like traditional bicycles, requiring riders to be 16 and wear helmets on public ways.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police issued a warning about a scammer impersonating Tom Brady after a local family was defrauded.
The victim sent gift cards to the scammer, believing they were corresponding with the iconic former Patriots quarterback.
Authorities recommend not sending money or gift cards to unknown persons as part of imposter scam prevention.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, from Cambridge is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room.
The Children's Room supports families and children through grief, providing understanding and emotional healing.
Castaneda is inspired by the Boston community's kindness and is running to give back to this compassionate organization.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga's Mayhem Ball tour at TD Garden in Boston featured a three-hour performance with songs from her new album.
Fans coordinated a surprise tribute with signs that brought Gaga to tears, celebrating her 40th birthday at the concert.
The performance included a mix of hits and new songs, highlighting Gaga's connection with fans during emotional moments.

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines plans to introduce Relax Row, a couch-style seating option, in economy class by 2027.
61% of over 100 respondents in an informal poll expressed eagerness to try United’s new seating concept.
Some readers expressed concerns about cost, privacy, and potential inconveniences with the Relax Row seating.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a teacher from Weymouth, is running the Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
Salani works at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy Lower Mills, where the foundation benefits her students directly.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation supports children in Boston, resonating with the school's mission of student excellence.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sand Beach at Acadia National Park in Maine is listed among America's best beaches for 2026 by Conde Nast Traveler.
The sandy beach is noted for its mix of pale shell sand, granite cliffs, and cold Atlantic waters.
Visitors are recommended to stay at Under Canvas Acadia or The Asticou Hotel, highlighted for their unique accommodations.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old was arrested for a shooting on Brighton Avenue in Allston that injured another juvenile.
Charges include assault with intent to murder, carrying a loaded firearm without a license, and discharging a firearm.
The shooting occurred Thursday evening, and the victim was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries.
